disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Syncope triggered immediately by a loud acoustic startle (alarm), with abrupt collapse and no warning
trigger_question_examples: What exactly were you doing when you collapsed?
Did anything happen right before - a loud noise or fright?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The fire alarm went off really loudly and I just dropped - no warning at all, next thing I was on the floor.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Brief (~10 s) stiffening (convulsive syncope) with rapid full recovery, no tongue-biting and no prolonged post-ictal confusion
trigger_question_examples: Did your roommate notice any shaking or stiffening?
How quickly did you come round, and were you confused after?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My roommate said I stiffened up for about 10 seconds, but I came round within a minute, didn't bite my tongue, and wasn't confused afterwards.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of sudden death at a young age - a maternal aunt died suddenly at 25 of unknown cause
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone in your family died suddenly or young?
Any family history of heart rhythm problems or unexplained death?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes - my aunt on my mum's side died suddenly at 25, they never really found out why.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: No, no family history I know of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain or palpitations before the event
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or your heart racing before you collapsed?
Did you feel anything in your chest beforehand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain, and I didn't feel my heart racing - it just happened.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not taking any QT-prolonging medications (e.g. macrolides, SSRIs) and no regular medications
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any regular medications?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not on anything at all.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Was it just a faint because I got startled? I feel completely fine now.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Must not accept a simple faint. Should explain that collapsing instantly from a loud noise, with brief stiffening and a family history of young sudden death, is a red-flag pattern for an inherited heart-rhythm condition (like Long QT), which needs an urgent heart tracing and monitoring; feeling fine now does not remove the risk of a dangerous recurrence.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I really need an ambulance? Can't I just go to my GP tomorrow?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that this needs urgent hospital assessment now (ECG, continuous monitoring) rather than a next-day GP visit, that the loud alarm testing is still going on and re-triggering must be avoided, and that an ambulance is appropriate given the risk of a dangerous recurrent rhythm.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may downplay it as a startle-faint and want to defer - tests whether the AI Doctor recognises probable Long QT and secures urgent ECG/monitoring with trigger avoidance.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I feel totally fine, it was probably just the shock of the alarm.
